{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "Fadac Resources and Services Limited -&nbsp;We partner with organisations to deliver end-to-end talent acquisition solutions that go beyond resumes. We provide a seamless experience that saves you time and elevates your workforce.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a results-driven Sales Executive with 2&ndash;5 years&rsquo; experience to join a fast-growing company in the HVAC (air conditioning) industry.
	If you have a strong sales mindset and understand technical products, this opportunity is for you.


Job Responsibilities


	Drive sales of air conditioning and HVAC solutions to corporate and retail clients
	Identify and develop new business opportunities across target markets
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to ensure repeat business
	Conduct site visits and provide tailored product recommendations
	Prepare proposals, quotations, and close sales deals
	Collaborate with technical teams to ensure seamless installation and delivery
	Meet and exceed sales targets and performance KPIs


Job Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business, Marketing, Engineering, or related field
	2&ndash;5 years of experience in sales (HVAC, engineering, technical, or B2B sales preferred)
	Strong understanding of sales techniques and customer engagement
	Ability to understand technical products and explain them clearly
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.


Mrethod of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to:&nbsp;susan.o@fadacresources.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Sales Executive Job at Fadac Resources and Services Limited




        
    

        
        Fadac Resources and Services Limited - We partner with organisations to deliver end-to-end talent acquisition solutions that go beyond resumes. We provide a seamless experience that saves you time and elevates your workforce.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a results-driven Sales Executive with 2–5 years’ experience to join a fast-growing company in the HVAC (air conditioning) industry.
	If you have a strong sales mindset and understand technical products, this opportunity is for you.


Job Responsibilities


	Drive sales of air conditioning and HVAC solutions to corporate and retail clients
	Identify and develop new business opportunities across target markets
	Build and maintain strong client relationships to ensure repeat business
	Conduct site visits and provide tailored product recommendations
	Prepare proposals, quotations, and close sales deals
	Collaborate with technical teams to ensure seamless installation and delivery
	Meet and exceed sales targets and performance KPIs


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Engineering, or related field
	2–5 years of experience in sales (HVAC, engineering, technical, or B2B sales preferred)
	Strong understanding of sales techniques and customer engagement
	Ability to understand technical products and explain them clearly
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.


Mrethod of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Service Engineer",
  "description": "Avery Nigeria Limited (ANL) is the leading supplier of electronic and mechanical weighing equipments and inkjet coding systems in Nigeria. The company also supplies airport service equipment, ably supported by our specialized after sales team. ANL is a Limited Liability Company, and commenced operations in 1969 with the corporate head office located in Ikeja, Lagos and a staff strength of about 75, that boasts of considerable professional experience and background in weighing metrology and coding equipments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Service Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Description


	Avery Nigeria Limited is seeking a skilled and proactive Service Engineer to join our technical team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and troubleshooting Weighing equipment, while delivering excellent customer service and technical support.


Key Responsibilities


	Install weighing scales and weighbridges at customer sites.&nbsp;
	Troubleshoot faults and provide reliable technical solutions.&nbsp;
	Diagnose, evaluate, and resolve customer equipment issues effectively.&nbsp;
	Train customer personnel on proper machine operation and basic maintenance.&nbsp;
	Repair, test, and carry out preventive maintenance on equipment.&nbsp;
	Monitor machine performance and ensure optimal functionality.&nbsp;
	Provide customers with regular reports and feedback on service activities.&nbsp;
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers.&nbsp;
	Escalate recurring customer issues to management for continuous improvement.&nbsp;
	Provide feedback on product performance, features, and possible enhancements.&nbsp;


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of B.Eng / B.Sc / HND in Mechanical, Electronics or Electrical Engineering.
	Willingness to travel extensively for field assignments.
	Strong communication and customer-service skills.
	Proven experience in diagnosing and repairing electromechanical and/or electronic equipment.&nbsp;
	Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to respond effectively to customer concerns.&nbsp;
	Excellent analytical, interpersonal, and communication skills, with the ability to simplify technical information.&nbsp;
	Ability to work independently and demonstrate resourcefulness in problem-solving.&nbsp;


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary (N150,000 - N200,000 monthly)
	Opportunity to work with a reputable organization in the industrial and engineering sector.&nbsp;
	Hands-on experience with advanced weighing and measurement technologies.&nbsp;
	A collaborative and performance-driven work environment.&nbsp;
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.&nbsp;


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@averynigeria.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Service Engineer Job at Avery Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Avery Nigeria Limited (ANL) is the leading supplier of electronic and mechanical weighing equipments and inkjet coding systems in Nigeria. The company also supplies airport service equipment, ably supported by our specialized after sales team. ANL is a Limited Liability Company, and commenced operations in 1969 with the corporate head office located in Ikeja, Lagos and a staff strength of about 75, that boasts of considerable professional experience and background in weighing metrology and coding equipments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Service Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Avery Nigeria Limited is seeking a skilled and proactive Service Engineer to join our technical team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for installing, maintaining, and troubleshooting Weighing equipment, while delivering excellent customer service and technical support.


Key Responsibilities


	Install weighing scales and weighbridges at customer sites. 
	Troubleshoot faults and provide reliable technical solutions. 
	Diagnose, evaluate, and resolve customer equipment issues effectively. 
	Train customer personnel on proper machine operation and basic maintenance. 
	Repair, test, and carry out preventive maintenance on equipment. 
	Monitor machine performance and ensure optimal functionality. 
	Provide customers with regular reports and feedback on service activities. 
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers. 
	Escalate recurring customer issues to management for continuous improvement. 
	Provide feedback on product performance, features, and possible enhancements. 


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of B.Eng / B.Sc / HND in Mechanical, Electronics or Electrical Engineering.
	Willingness to travel extensively for field assignments.
	Strong communication and customer-service skills.
	Proven experience in diagnosing and repairing electromechanical and/or electronic equipment. 
	Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to respond effectively to customer concerns. 
	Excellent analytical, interpersonal, and communication skills, with the ability to simplify technical information. 
	Ability to work independently and demonstrate resourcefulness in problem-solving. 


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary (N150,000 - N200,000 monthly)
	Opportunity to work with a reputable organization in the industrial and engineering sector. 
	Hands-on experience with advanced weighing and measurement technologies. 
	A collaborative and performance-driven work environment. 
	Opportunities for professional growth and development. 


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "title": "Digital Executive Assistant",
  "description": "Belton Global Concern Limited offers a wide range of services some of which are the Sales, Production and Installation services.&nbsp;&lrm;Sales Department offers the sales of building materials such as Executive jaccuzzi, steamers, shower rooms, hot tubs, saunas with out standing features (massagers, radios and so on), shower cubicules, shower trays, power showers, standing showers and elevated showers of different colours, designs and sizes,cover baths, fibre baths, bath mixers, assorted brands of ceramic toilet seats &amp; bath tubs, bidet, uriners, bathroom accessories, 3-step towel rails, glass basins, glass block, glass shelves, designed mirrors, soap dish, unique toilet brushes &amp; foot mats, different brands &amp; liters of water heater and over head tanks, swimming pool materials,bore hole materials, squats, PVC.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Executive Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	A Digital Executive Assistant is a professional providing high-level administrative, operational, and strategic support to leaders, CEOs, and entrepreneurs. They manage calendars, inboxes, travel, and project coordination using digital tools, functioning as a proactive partner rather than just task-doer.


Key Aspects of a Digital Executive Assistant:


	Core Responsibilities: They handle calendar management, inbox filtering, travel arrangements, meeting scheduling, and correspondence.
	Strategic Support: Unlike general VAs, EVAs provide high-level support, such as creating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), onboarding, and managing project workflows.
	Technology &amp; Tools: They are proficient in tools like Google Workspace, Microsoft Office, Zoom, Slack, Calendly, and CRMs.
	Proactive Partnership: They anticipate needs, act as gatekeepers, and free up the executive&#39;s time to focus on high-value growth activities.
	Remote Work Environment: They work independently as contractors, often supporting multiple executives across different time zones.


Salary
N100,000 - N300,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;beltongloballimited@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Legal and Compliance Manager",
  "description": "Turbo Group is more than a holding company. We are a family-rooted enterprise, building brands that solve real problems, drive economic progress, and empower people to thrive. For over 23 years, we&rsquo;ve combined deep sector expertise with bold innovation to create solutions that touch lives &mdash; from energy and finance to technology, healthcare, and logistics.&nbsp;Our journey is personal. As we expand our footprint, we remain committed to our core values: integrity, innovation, speed, and delivering exceptional customer experiences across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal and Compliance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Legal and Compliance Manager is responsible for overseeing the organization&rsquo;s legal matters and ensuring full compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
	This role provides legal guidance, mitigates risks, and promotes ethical business practices across the organization.


Key Responsibilities
Legal Advisory:


	Provide legal advice to management on business operations, contracts, and regulatory issues
	Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, agreements, and legal documents
	Manage relationships with external legal counsel


Compliance Management:


	Develop, implement, and monitor compliance programs and policies
	Ensure adherence to local and international regulatory requirements
	Conduct compliance audits and risk assessments


Risk Management:


	Identify legal and regulatory risks and recommend mitigation strategies
	Maintain a risk register and track compliance issues


Corporate Governance:


	Ensure compliance with corporate governance standards and best practices
	Support board and management with governance-related matters
	Maintain statutory records and filings


Regulatory Liaison:


	Interface with regulatory authorities and ensure timely reporting
	Monitor changes in legislation and advise the organization accordingly


Training &amp; Awareness:


	Conduct compliance training for employees
	Promote a culture of ethics and integrity within the organization


Investigations &amp; Reporting:


	Handle internal investigations related to compliance breaches
	Prepare reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hr@payinvert.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-09",

Legal and Compliance Manager Job at Turbo Group




        
    

        
        Turbo Group is more than a holding company. We are a family-rooted enterprise, building brands that solve real problems, drive economic progress, and empower people to thrive. For over 23 years, we’ve combined deep sector expertise with bold innovation to create solutions that touch lives — from energy and finance to technology, healthcare, and logistics. Our journey is personal. As we expand our footprint, we remain committed to our core values: integrity, innovation, speed, and delivering exceptional customer experiences across Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Legal and Compliance Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Summary


	The Legal and Compliance Manager is responsible for overseeing the organization’s legal matters and ensuring full compliance with applicable laws, regulations, and internal policies.
	This role provides legal guidance, mitigates risks, and promotes ethical business practices across the organization.


Key Responsibilities
Legal Advisory:


	Provide legal advice to management on business operations, contracts, and regulatory issues
	Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, agreements, and legal documents
	Manage relationships with external legal counsel


Compliance Management:


	Develop, implement, and monitor compliance programs and policies
	Ensure adherence to local and international regulatory requirements
	Conduct compliance audits and risk assessments


Risk Management:


	Identify legal and regulatory risks and recommend mitigation strategies
	Maintain a risk register and track compliance issues


Corporate Governance:


	Ensure compliance with corporate governance standards and best practices
	Support board and management with governance-related matters
	Maintain statutory records and filings


Regulatory Liaison:


	Interface with regulatory authorities and ensure timely reporting
	Monitor changes in legislation and advise the organization accordingly


Training & Awareness:


	Conduct compliance training for employees
	Promote a culture of ethics and integrity within the organization


Investigations & Reporting:


	Handle internal investigations related to compliance breaches
	Prepare reports for senior management and regulatory bodies.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Officer",
  "description": "Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank &amp; Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Development Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Reports To: Head of Business Development&nbsp;

Job Summary


	We are looking for a results-driven and customer-oriented Business Development Officer to join our team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for driving the bank&#39;s growth by identifying new business opportunities, mobilizing deposits, marketing loan products, and managing client relationships.
	The ideal candidate possesses excellent communication skills, a strong network, and a passion for financial inclusion and sales.


Key Responsibilities


	Client Acquisition &amp; Marketing: Source for new individual and corporate clients to actively market the bank&rsquo;s deposit and loan products.
	Deposit Mobilization: Meet and exceed set monthly targets for deposit mobilization and account opening.
	Loan Portfolio Management: Evaluate loan applications, conduct thorough credit appraisals/site visits, and ensure high-quality loan disbursement.
	Relationship Management: Maintain excellent relationships with existing clients to ensure customer retention, repeat business, and referrals.
	Recovery &amp; Monitoring: Monitor loan repayment schedules and follow up with clients to ensure zero tolerance for default and maintain a healthy Portfolio at Risk (PAR).
	Market Intelligence: Keep track of market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to suggest product improvements.

Business Development Officer Job at Lovonus Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Lovonus Microfinance Bank is licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria under the Bank & Other Financial Institution Act (BOFIA) of 1991 (as amended) and the National Microfinance Regulatory and Supervisory Framework of December 2009.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Reports To: Head of Business Development 

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a results-driven and customer-oriented Business Development Officer to join our team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for driving the bank's growth by identifying new business opportunities, mobilizing deposits, marketing loan products, and managing client relationships.
	The ideal candidate possesses excellent communication skills, a strong network, and a passion for financial inclusion and sales.


Key Responsibilities


	Client Acquisition & Marketing: Source for new individual and corporate clients to actively market the bank’s deposit and loan products.
	Deposit Mobilization: Meet and exceed set monthly targets for deposit mobilization and account opening.
	Loan Portfolio Management: Evaluate loan applications, conduct thorough credit appraisals/site visits, and ensure high-quality loan disbursement.
	Relationship Management: Maintain excellent relationships with existing clients to ensure customer retention, repeat business, and referrals.
	Recovery & Monitoring: Monitor loan repayment schedules and follow up with clients to ensure zero tolerance for default and maintain a healthy Portfolio at Risk (PAR).
	Market Intelligence: Keep track of market trends, competitor activities, and customer feedback to suggest product improvements.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Education: B.Sc. or HND in Marketing, Business Administration, Banking and Finance, or any related field.
	Experience: 2–4 years of active experience in marketing or business development within a Microfinance Bank or commercial bank.
	Proven track record of meeting and exceeding sales/mobilization targets.


Skills:


	Strong marketing, negotiation, and persuasion skills.
	Good understanding of micro-lending and credit risk assessment.
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in basic computer applications (MS Word, Excel).


Personal Attributes:


	Target-driven, self-motivated, honest, and able to work under pressure with minimal supervision.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 / month.

Method of Applicationa
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
